title: syzygium nervosum var. paniala
intro: Syzygium nervosum var. paniala, also known as the Paniala or Nilambur Teak, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Paniala include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Paniala is often used in the form of a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and bark are used in traditional medicine to create various remedies, such as teas, tonics, and ointments.
